title: U.S. Luxury Resale Market Outlook Report 2025-2030
text: The U.S. luxury resale market was valued at USD 8.65 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 13.04 billion by 2030, representing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.08%. The luxury resale market is expected to grow 2-3x faster than the first-hand luxury market from 2025 to 2027. The RealReal, eBay, and ThreadUp are identified as the key players in the luxury resale market. Key growth drivers include changing consumer preferences toward sustainability and value, inflation reducing first-hand luxury purchasing power, and increasing adoption of resale by affluent consumers. The report highlights that nearly 47% of luxury consumers now consider resale value in purchasing decisions. Fine jewelry, designer handbags, luxury watches, and designer apparel remain the strongest categories. The RealReal's market position is strengthened by its focus on premium consignment, authentication infrastructure, and customer experience. The company's supply-unlocking initiatives through retail stores and partnerships position it well to capture growing demand in the expanding luxury resale market. Q1 2026 performance with 24% GMV growth and 15% AOV growth demonstrates the company's ability to benefit from market tailwinds.
